message: Corn dog batter broke down by hour three yesterday. We're starting over on the ratios - cornmeal to flour, oil content, everything. Non-negotiable today.
Yeah, totally - let's nail the ratios this time so we're not chasing our tails again.
Good. We need the emulsifier ratio tested first - that's where yesterday fell apart.
The emulsifier is the skeleton - get that right and the rest of the story follows. I'm curious whether you're leaning egg or something gentler for the mouthfeel.
Egg's probably safer for binding - gentler than you'd think - but maybe we test both and see which one holds through hour four?
Egg's the safer bet for hour four - it'll hold better than lecithin would through the temperature swings. Test both though, see which one keeps that emulsion stable when it matters.
